spec_match.rationale: Requests a floating-point backing store via getContext('2d', { colorSpace, colorType: 'float16' }) — the exact API the spec adds.

interactive.rationale: Buttons toggle between unorm8 and float16 backing stores and re-render the same scene; visible difference where supported.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb describes a 2D canvas requesting a floating-point backing store; that is what the page does.

edge_cases.rationale: Missing: graceful detection on engines that ignore the colorType option, readback via getImageData with the new Float16Array, and out-of-gamut colour clipping.

references.rationale: The see-also section now links to the correct ChromeStatus feature 5086141338877952.

design_system.rationale: All variables, canvas pixel values are content not design tokens.

openQuestions.title: Add a getImageData(Float16Array) lane
openQuestions.detail: The new colorType lets `getImageData` return a Float16Array. Add a button that reads back a pixel and prints the raw float values so authors see the wider gamut representation.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-detection probe with fallback
openQuestions.detail: If the context option is ignored, the canvas silently uses unorm8 and the visual difference is zero — confusing. Probe with a known-out-of-gamut colour and surface a clear `float16 not supported in this browser` banner when it falls back.

summary: Faithful demo of the new API; the remaining gaps are Float16Array readback and clearer fallback detection when the float16 context is ignored.
